A just case is better argued with gentleness than with strong emotion. Nothing provokes anger like harsh words.
Whoever has knowledge should use it properly for the benefit of others.
Hidden sins, actions, and sadnesses are under God’s eye. This brings comfort to believers and fear to those who sin.
A good tongue heals wounded consciences by comforting them; it convinces sin-sick souls; and it reconciles people who are in disagreement.
If instruction is despised, correct people instead of letting them continue unchecked toward destruction.
The wealth of worldly people increases their fears and suspicions, strengthens their passions, and makes the fear of death more distressing.
We use knowledge correctly when we share it, but a foolish person’s heart has nothing good to share.
People who do wrong substitute other things for Christ’s sacrifice, or for righteous behavior. Spiritual gifts are his provision, and the work of his Spirit, which pleases him greatly.
Whoever hates correction will perish in their sins, because they were unwilling to let them go.
There is nothing that can be hidden from the eyes of God, not even people’s thoughts.
A scoffer cannot bear to reflect seriously within his own heart.
A gloomy, impatient, and ungrateful spirit, arising from pride and excessive fondness for earthly possessions, makes a person restless with themselves and with those around them.
A wise person seeks to gain more wisdom, growing in grace and in the knowledge of Christ. But a worldly mind is content, flattering itself.

Some people experience great distress and have a sorrowful spirit. These individuals are to be pitied, prayed for, and comforted. Others serve God with joy in their hearts, and that inspires their obedience, but they should still rejoice with reverence.
Believers often possess enough when people see little; the Lord is with them, without the worries, troubles, and temptations that come with the wealth of the wicked.
Whoever is slow to anger does not only prevent conflict but calms it down if it has started.

People who lack enthusiasm for their work claim that they cannot do it without difficulty and risk. Therefore, many remain constantly uncertain about their standing because they consistently fail to fulfill some responsibility.
People who treat an older mother or father with disrespect or neglect reveal their own foolishness.
Those who are genuinely wise strive to ensure that their thoughts, words, and actions are consistent, honest, and sacred.
If people do not take the time and effort to consider something carefully, they are unlikely to accomplish anything.
Wisdom is needed to adjust our speech to the circumstances.
A good person focuses their desires on things above; their path leads straight there.
Pride ruins many people. But God will support those who are in distress.
The thoughts of wicked people offend God who knows the heart.
The greedy person allows no member of their family any rest or enjoyment. And a desire for wealth frequently leads to schemes that result in destruction.
A good person is proven to be a wise person by this: he controls his speech well.
God distances himself from people who defy him.
How delightful it is for a humble person to hear the good news of salvation through the Lord Jesus Christ!
Faithful, friendly corrections help spiritual life and lead to eternal life.
Sinners do not value their own souls; therefore they prefer the body over the soul and harm the soul to please the body.

A reverent search of the Scriptures and following the guidance of the Holy Spirit come from a fear of the Lord. As we depend entirely on the grace of God, we are elevated by the righteousness of Christ.
